Evaluate forecasts with rolling-origin backtesting

Source: R/evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast.R
evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast.Rd

Repeatedly refits forecast_poisson_growth() on expanding windows and evaluates short-term out-of-sample performance.

Usage

evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast(
  data,
  date_col = "date",
  count_col = "incidence",
  initial_window,
  horizon = 7L,
  step = 1L,
  level = 0.9,
  n_draws = 1000L,
  include_observation_uncertainty = TRUE,
  mean_si = 4.7,
  wis_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

data

A data frame with date and incidence columns.

date_col

Name of date column.

count_col

Name of incidence/count column.

initial_window

Initial training-window length.

horizon

Forecast horizon in days.

step

Step size between forecast origins.

level

Central prediction interval level for coverage.

n_draws

Number of forecast draws per origin.

include_observation_uncertainty

Whether to include Poisson observation uncertainty in forecast draws.

mean_si

Mean serial interval passed to forecast_poisson_growth().

wis_levels

Central interval levels used in WIS.

seed

Optional random seed.

Value

A list with:

  • scores: day-level scores by origin/day,

  • metrics_overall: aggregated overall metrics,

  • metrics_by_horizon: metrics aggregated by forecast horizon day,

  • forecast_summaries: concatenated forecast summaries.
